# QZX - Quick Zap Exchange
> Verbose is Gold. Consistent cross-platform CLI diagnostics.

QZX is a cross-platform command-line tool designed to provide identical outputs and JSON formatting across Windows, macOS, and Linux.

## Philosophy: Verbose is Gold
Traditional Unix commands favor silence on success. QZX operates under the **"Verbose is Gold"** philosophy: every command returns a structured JSON payload with rich metadata and human-readable context. Responses always contain at least:
```json
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Descriptive status message",
  "details": { ... extra records ... }
}
```

---

## Installation & Usage

### CLI Installation
Install globally using npm/pnpm:
```bash
npm install -g qzx-cli
# or
pnpm add -g qzx-cli
```

### Basic Syntax
```bash
qzx <command> [arguments] [options]
```
Add `--json` anywhere in the command arguments to force raw JSON output (optimal for AI agents and automation scripts).

---

## Complete Command Index

### 📁 File Commands
*   **`listFiles`** `[path] [pattern]` - Lists files in a directory with wildcard matching and details.
*   **`findFiles`** `[path] [pattern]` - Deep search for files.
*   **`detectFileType`** `[file_path]` - Analyzes binary magic bytes to determine file type/MIME.
*   **`countLinesInFile`** `[file_path]` - Counts lines, empty lines, and comments in a text file.
*   **`getFileHash`** `[file_path] [algorithm]` - Calculates MD5, SHA-1, or SHA-256 checksums.
*   **`compressZip`** `[zip_path] [source_path] [exclude_patterns]` - Creates a ZIP archive recursively.
*   **`decompressZip`** `[zip_path] [target_path]` - Safely extracts a ZIP, preventing directory traversal.
*   **`findDuplicateFiles`** `[scan_path] [min_size_kb] [max_depth]` - Groups and lists duplicate files.
*   **`findBrokenSymlinks`** `[folder_path] [max_depth]` - Identifies broken shortcuts and symbolic links.
*   **`inspectImage`** `[image_path]` - Extracts dimensions and metadata from PNG/JPEG/GIF/BMP.
*   **`read_file`**, **`copy_file`**, **`move_file`**, **`delete_file`**, **`touch_file`**, **`create_directory`** - Standard filesystem operations with uniform JSON outcomes.

### 🌐 Network Commands
*   **`checkUrlStatus`** `[url] [timeout]` - Pings a URL to check HTTP status, code, headers, and online status.
*   **`checkDns`** `[domain]` - Queries A, AAAA, MX, TXT, NS, and CNAME records.
*   **`checkSslCertificate`** `[host] [port]` - Inspects SSL/TLS expiration dates, domain matching, and cipher parameters.
*   **`getNetworkConfig`** `[check_public]` - Lists local IPs, adapters, default gateway, DNS servers, and checks for VPN.
*   **`testWebSpeed`** `[max_seconds]` - Benchmarks download speed (Mbps) and HTTP latency (ms).

### 💻 System Commands
*   **`SystemInfo`** - Displays operating system details, RAM size, CPU model, python version, and user context.
*   **`getEnvironmentInfo`** - Retrieves detailed hardware usage reports (RAM, Storage, GPU load).
*   **`inspectPort`** `[port] [kill]` - Identifies processes utilizing a port and can optionally kill them.
*   **`getStartupPrograms`** - Lists apps configured to run at boot (Windows Registry / Unix Autostart).
*   **`testDiskSpeed`** `[test_path] [size_mb]` - Benchmarks sequential read and write speeds.
*   **`listSystemServices`** `[status]` - Inventories running or stopped OS background services.
*   **`list_processes`**, **`kill_process`** - Monitors and manages active processes.
*   **`isAdmin`** - Assesses if the shell runs with root/Administrator permissions.
*   **`clear_screen`**, **`current_dir`**, **`version`** - Basic environmental shell utilities.

### 🛠️ Development Commands
*   **`getGitStatus`** `[repo_path]` - Summarizes git branch, upstream sync, modified files, and recent history.
*   **`scanProject`** `[project_path]` - Audits tech stacks, package managers, scripts, and checks `.env` variables compared to `.env.example`.
*   **`getProjectTree`** `[dir_path] [max_depth]` - Displays ASCII directory layouts excluding build/temp folders.
*   **`analyze_complexity`** `[file_path]` - Rates cyclomatic complexity of Python source files.
*   **`compare_files`** `[file_a] [file_b]` - Performs side-by-side file diff comparisons.
*   **`scaffold_python`**, **`scaffold_rust`**, **`scaffold_cpp`**, **`scaffold_c`** - Standardizes project scaffolds.

---

For full details, interactive demos, and documentation, visit: https://qzx.yumbale.com
